channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The NetSapiens inter-domain calling quirk that keeps showing up
item.description: Quick share. Been running into this enough times across NetSapiens deployments that it's worth flagging. Scenario: Call gets placed or transferred between two domains on the same NetSapiens...

item.title: Game Jams no Browser: Você Não Precisa de Unity
item.description: Se você já fez algum front-end interativo, já tem 80% do que precisa pra fazer um jogo simples. Game jams como a June Solstice são desculpa perfeita pra testar isso — três semanas, tema aberto, e...

item.title: 10 JSON Errors Every Developer Hits (And Exactly How to Fix Them)
item.description: JSON looks simple — just key-value pairs, right? But its strictness is legendary. No trailing commas. No comments. No single quotes. No undefined. Miss one rule and your entire payload is dead on...

item.title: Why "Available on All Platforms" Usually Means You're Excluded
item.description: AI tools keep promising to democratize access - but if your team runs Linux, you already know that promise has some fine print. The Quiet Gap Between "For Everyone" and What Ships There's a phrase...

item.title: My 100-days-of-solana experience so far
item.description: My experience honestly has been fun .. learning about the Solana blockchain, how to build on it and everything in between. the concept which has stuck with me the most is this: For Traditional...

item.title: Marketing agents need workflow boundaries, not better prompts
item.description: On June 6, 2026, I spent the day at Profound HQ in New York City as a selected participant and solo builder at the Marketing Engineering Hackathon. I wrote the canonical field note here: Profound...

item.title: Build A Basic AI Agent From Scratch: Long Task Planning
item.description: In the previous part of the Build A Basic AI Agent From Scratch series, we added the essential tools to our agent to allow it to work autonomously for us. We gave it the ability to find files,...

item.title: Gubernator E101 examples examples examplesss....
item.description: Gubernator Examples: Hands-On Tutorials Gubernator features four progressive developer examples designed to walk you from basic container orchestration to advanced AI workloads: Example 101 —...

item.title: Building a Broadcast Clock: The Math Behind Radio Station Programming Schedules
item.description: Building a Broadcast Clock: The Math Behind Radio Station Programming Schedules By the KAVANA engineering team -- June 2026 The broadcast clock defines what happens in each minute of an hour: news...

item.title: Building an agentic Jira automation platform with MCP and Temporal
item.description: Most "AI automation" demos fall apart the moment a workflow needs to run longer than a single request. An agent makes a few tool calls, the process crashes or times out, and you lose all state. I...

item.title: The Download: how the World Cup ball will fly and OpenAI’s “super app”
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why this year’s World Cup ball may not fly as far Much is...

item.title: Building Pakistan Notice Helper: A Small AI Tool for a Very Local Safety Problem
Uber tells London to get ready for robotaxis
item.description: Uber is getting ready to put robotaxis on London's streets, opening an interest list for riders who want to be among the first to hail one of Wayve's autonomous vehicles when the service goes live...

item.title: Anthropic’s Project Glasswing Update
item.description: In April, Anthropic initated Project Glasswing. The idea was to let companies use their new model to find and fix vulnerabilities in their own software. It was a fantastic PR move, and so many...

item.title: WWDC 2026: How to watch and what to expect
item.description: Apple's biggest event of the year is nearly here. The company's Worldwide Developers Conference will spotlight updates to iOS, macOS, and all of Apple's other operating systems, and this year's...

item.title: Momfluencers Are Pitching AI as a Better ‘Coparent’ Than Men
item.description: Moms are outsourcing tedious household tasks to ChatGPT and selling courses teaching others to do the same. Where are all the dads?

item.title: VerdantBamboo Deploys BSD Variant of BRICKSTORM on Linux Appliances
item.description: A China-nexus cyber espionage group has been observed deploying a BSD variant of a known backdoor called BRICKSTORM, as well as two other malware families codenamed PLENET (aka GRIMBOLT) and...

item.title: Why this year’s World Cup ball may not fly as far
item.description: Much is new about this month’s upcoming FIFA World Cup tournament, which will be held in the US, Canada, and Mexico. It hosts more teams than ever before. It’s the first to occur in three...

item.title: UNC3753 Used Vishing and Physical Intrusions in U.S. Data Theft Extortion Campaign
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a financially motivated data theft extortion campaign that has targeted dozens of organizations across professional, legal, and financial...

item.title: VS Code Adds 2-Hour Extension Auto-Update Delay to Limit Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Microsoft has announced that Visual Studio Code (VS Code) will apply a two-hour delay before extensions for the integrated development environment (IDE) are updated automatically to a newer...

item.title: NASA will wear high-tech Prada long johns to the Moon
item.description: We've seen Axiom Space and Prada's collaboration on the Axiom Extravehicular Mobility Unit (AxEMU) spacesuit. Now the company has revealed the Liquid Cooling and Ventilation Garment (LCVG) that...

item.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
item.description: We're likely to see more price increases as the big AI companies plan to go public.

item.title: The 7 biggest storylines from Summer Game Fest 2026
item.description: The 2026 edition of Summer Game Fest just wrapped up, and it was surprisingly hectic. The nearly week-long event came at a challenging time for the games industry, and for the most part the big...

item.title: Persona 6 exists, and that’s all we know
item.description: This year's Summer Game Fest turned out to be a good one for fans of Japanese RPGs. First, the conclusion of the Final Fantasy VII remake trilogy was announced at SGF Live, and now we have the...

item.title: Notion restores access to Anthropic after service disruption
item.description: Notion's head of product said he was "astonished" at “the amount of people RT-ing this."

item.title: Microsoft’s Xbox 25th anniversary console comes in translucent green
item.description: Microsoft has created a special edition Xbox Series X to celebrate 25 years of the console. The Xbox 25th-anniversary console takes design cues from the original Xbox console, with both the...

item.title: Minecraft Dungeons 2 gets a September release date
item.description: Minecraft Dungeons 2, a sequel to Microsoft's dungeon crawler spinoff Minecraft Dungeons, will be released on September 29th. The company originally revealed the game in a brief trailer in March,...

item.title: Fable launches in late February after recent delay
item.description: Just a few days after pushing Fable out of 2026, Microsoft showed off more footage of Fable, the first new entry in the storied RPG franchise since 2010's Fable III, at its Xbox Games Showcase on...

item.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved arrives July 28th
item.description: As part of its Xbox Games Showcase on Sunday, Microsoft revealed new details about Halo: Campaign Evolved, the upcoming remake of Halo: Combat Evolved's campaign mode. The remake will debut on...

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day isn’t coming to the PS5
item.description: Apparently, the "return of Xbox" means a retreat from other platforms. At its Xbox Games Showcase today, Microsoft revealed that Gears of War: E-Day - which was previously rumored for a PS5 launch...
